Patch Set 1:

This looks like something that would cause oopsed on older servers, but I guess these locks are only requested between MDTs?  That avoids the need for protocol compatibility with clients I think, but it looks like this patch should also be backported to older released after it lands to master.

Patch Set 1:

Yes, this is only for locks between MDTs. And this patch should be port to 2.5 and 2.7.

Patch Set 4:

"Is there a test case that could be added to verify this?"

I will add it in the next patch, accessing the files under migrating directory.
i.e. one thread keep migrating directory among different MDTs, and another thread access the directory at the same time and no failure should happen. 

This test case will verify this.

parents.subject: LU-6581 mdt: Transfer layout only if layout lock is granted

commit.subject: LU-6473 mdt: enqueue lookup lock on the parent MDT
commit.message:
LU-6473 mdt: enqueue lookup lock on the parent MDT

Because the parent MDT will control the LOOKUP lock,
so Unlink and migration should enqueue the LOOKUP lock
on the parent MDT.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I68fa328dc0e5f255b6a5b99a22db90a722c415fa
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14495
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
